meta.objective: Turn every course home into an attractive marketing-style page that quickly explains why the learner should care and complete the course, with minimal instructional framing.

tasks.title: Turn course homes into compelling landing pages
tasks.objective: Make all five course homes attractive, concise, and motivation-led while preserving honest claims, navigation, and the course's editorial visual language.
acceptanceCriteria: Every course home leads with a course-specific reason to care and a clear start or continue action.
Readiness checks, prerequisite decisions, step-by-step orientation copy, inspectable traces, and audit-like source instructions are absent from the visible home flow.
Each home presents concise course-specific payoffs and an attractive preview of what learners will build.
Supporting provenance and evidence boundaries remain honest but visually secondary.
Desktop and narrow-screen browser QA, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
tasks.details: Refactor the shared HomeView and course hero metadata, update the home-page visual system and regression tests, verify all five course routes, and refresh reviewed page-grade evidence as required.

validationCommands.evidence: 29/29 focused tests passed before report regeneration.

validationCommands.command: browser QA on /llm/, /worldmodel/, /generative/, /rl/, /embodied/ at desktop and 390x844
validationCommands.evidence: All five homes had three payoffs, two CTAs, no forbidden readiness copy, no horizontal overflow, and no browser errors.
Static export and artifact/grade verification passed; 161/161 tests passed.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Every course home leads with a course-specific reason to care and a clear start or continue action.
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Five distinct campaign promises, reasons, finish lines, and two CTAs render through the shared home shell.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Readiness checks, prerequisite decisions, step-by-step orientation copy, inspectable traces, and audit-like source instructions are absent from the visible home flow.
acceptanceCheckResults.details: The obsolete renderer and CSS were removed; regressions forbid the prior home scaffolding and browser QA found none of its visible wording.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Each home presents concise course-specific payoffs and an attractive preview of what learners will build.
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Each course has three authored payoff cards, a marketing-led arc, course frontiers, stats, and a finish-line statement.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Supporting provenance and evidence boundaries remain honest but visually secondary.
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Generative and RL retain compact claim-linked source cards; every finale preserves the fixture, optional-run, and review-date boundary.

acceptanceCheckResults.criterion: Desktop and narrow-screen browser QA, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
acceptanceCheckResults.details: Browser desktop/mobile audits passed, lint passed, and the full static-export regression suite passed.

evidence: Independent home review passed with final scores LLM 98, World Models 98, Generative 99, RL 99, Embodied 98.
World Models phase-label mismatch was repaired with a six-label regression.
Commit f0fb090 contains the verified implementation.
